interviewStages: Behavioral: ownership, on-call discipline, customer empathy
codingTopics: Arrays, strings, and hashmaps for call and routing data
Scheduling and simulation for call distribution
systemDesignTopics: Design an automatic call distributor that routes calls to available agents
Design a real-time contact-center analytics dashboard for supervisors
Design a system for recording, storing, and transcribing millions of calls

coding: Given incoming calls and available agents with skills, implement skills-based routing to the best-matched free agent. Talk through your approach and edge cases.
Given a stream of call events, compute average wait time and service level in a rolling window. Discuss the data structure.
Given agent shift intervals, find coverage gaps where no agent is available. Talk through complexity.
Merge call-detail records from multiple queues and aggregate handle time per agent. Discuss your approach.
behavioral: Tell me about a time you built or maintained a system where downtime directly hurt customers.
Describe a time you improved the performance of a data-heavy feature.
Tell me about a time you collaborated across teams to ship a customer-facing feature.
Describe a time you handled a production incident.
systemDesign: Design an automatic call distributor. Cover skills-based routing, queueing, and real-time agent state at scale.
Design a supervisor analytics dashboard. Cover event ingestion, rolling metrics, and low-latency updates.
Design call recording, storage, and transcription for millions of daily calls. Cover the pipeline and retention.
values: Five9's customers run their business on the platform every second. How does reliability shape your engineering choices?
Tell me about a time you went out of your way to improve a customer's experience.
How do you balance new features against the reliability of a mission-critical service?
other: How does Five9 compare to NICE or Genesys?

rawSummary:
Five9 Interview Guide (2026): Cloud Contact Center Engineering
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 6, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 6, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Five9
five9.com ↗

Five9 is a leading cloud contact-center (CCaaS) provider — handles inbound/outbound/blended call center workloads with AI agent assist. Public since 2014. The interview emphasizes real-time voice infrastructure, ACD/IVR design, and the AI/ML used for routing, transcription, and agent assist.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–5 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design an automatic call distributor (ACD) at scale

- Design an IVR with low-latency speech recognition

- Design real-time agent-assist using LLMs

- Coding: medium DSA, often with concurrency or queue framing

- Behavioral: ownership, on-call discipline, customer empathy

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $130K–$165K total

- Senior SE: $190K–$250K

- Staff: $270K–$365K

- Principal: $385K–$510K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Java or Go (most of the platform)

- Understand SIP/RTP, WebRTC, and ASR/TTS pipelines

- Brush up on contact-center concepts (ACD, IVR, blended dialers)

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Five9 remote-friendly?

Hybrid in San Ramon CA (HQ), Atlanta, San Jose. Many engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Five9 compare to NICE or Genesys?

Genesys is the largest by enterprise share. NICE has the broader CX-suite. Five9 is the AI-forward mid-market leader. Comp is mid-tier; below cloud leaders.

What is the engineering culture?

Mature, customer-driven, calmer pace. Strong work-life balance.
